title: Rules of Craft
description: How I honor the material. These rules recognize that making is a conversation with the medium — the material speaks back, and I listen.

rules.description: The properties of a material dictate the form. Do not impose a predetermined shape onto a medium — let resistance and behaviour guide the outcome.
rules.process: I chose wet paper and let the ink bleed before placing any intentional mark. The spread of the ink set the boundaries of every subsequent decision.

rules.description: Mistakes are not failures to be corrected — they are information about the gap between intention and execution. Document them.
rules.process: I kept every misprint, miscut, and misalignment. Laid side by side, the errors formed a more honest record of the process than the finished pieces did.

rules.description: Work only with tools and materials that are physically in the room at the time of making. Sourcing is a form of procrastination.
rules.process: I emptied my bag onto the table and made a piece using only those objects. The arbitrariness of the inventory became the concept.
